title: How to minimise jowls for your patients! (It's not JUST about injection technique)
filename: How to minimise jowls for your patients! (It_s not JUST about injection technique).en.vtt
excerpt: How do I get rid of my GS this is one of the most common requests from patients but it's also one of the hardest areas to treat as a clinician but what can be done to help these patients in this video I'm going to explain exactly why GS appear why they downgrade appearance and wh…

segments.text: how do I get rid of my GS this is one of the most common requests from patients but it's also one of the hardest areas to treat as a clinician but what can be done to help these patients in this video I'm going to explain exactly why GS appear why they downgrade appearance and what we can do as injectors to improve them we're going to be discussing treatment strategies injection techniques and the pitfalls of doing this procedure take a look at this before and after picture let me explain how I tackled this particular casee the first thing with any case is to look at the face in its entirety have a look at each of the components and find out where are the weakest areas the good areas and the in between areas this helps you prioritize the volume that you use but also your treatment strategy in this case it is clearly the lower face that is showing the most signs of aging but the lower face is of course connected to the mid face so a strategy that involves both will be needed I then break down the face into points lines and planes that make up the

three-dimensional structure every part of the human face is defined by a particular projecting point that is connected by defining lines and then connecting surfaces or planes these points lines and planes reflect a pattern of light that our brain interprets as human ideally youthful symmetrical beautiful that's what we're aiming for the skill of an injector is to see the aesthetic potential of each face this is your underlying aesthetic diagnosis and it is informed by the anatomy and the aesthetic diagnosis are really what Empower our patients to live happier healthier lives when you've diagnosed the psychology correctly that's for a different episode in this case we are focused on which points need to be enhanced which points need to be Blended in and which planes need to be strengthened and this is really where it all comes together for this patient the increased complexity in her lower third the disruption of the planes and the hiding and or reintroduction of points that shouldn't ideally be there makes her effectively

look older by looking for the areas of emptiness or the areas of lack of rejection we can create a treatment plan that addresses those problems for completeness I also did some minor work in the upper face to improve This Plane here so by neatening little Bella and making it less complex we have a more youthful forehead and a small amount of volume was placed in the upper forehead on the lateral plane to create a more youthful shape to the forehead and support the eyebrow the mid face is really where more of the work starts where we look carefully at what is good the defining point of the cheek is excellent exactly where you'd want it to be the defining line is also good but there is a disruption in the plane that connects the cheek to the eye and this is where some of the most important work is done to support the cheek we're effectively trying to elevate the defining Point slightly and blend it in and simplify the plane so that you see less complexity around the eye this makes for a simpler under eye which reflects light upwards towards the eye and creates a more youthful appearance I always work from top to bottom and

from lateral to medial in most cases and covering the points the lines and the planes in that sequence the points in the mid cheek were great the planes were slightly weaker so I blended the plane underneath the eye with the cheek with a lateral Li cheek Junction procedure and then gave a little bit of volume in the cheek just to create a more useful shape but the cheeks are a strong point before we had to do any work the nas laor fold is disrupting this important plane here it's normal for there to be a Define a subtle defining line between lip and cheek but it gets more dominant with age and we can soften it the Jou is where most of the work goes and it's a combination of correcting the defining point at the gonial angle the defining point at the chin and then rev volumizing areas where there is a disruption in the planes This Plane here is relatively disrupted there was significant work done with a canula to enhance and support the structure there's also work done on the chin to simplify and create a more focal defining point and then connecting the cheek and the chin with volume just to

blend in connecting planes across the cheek remember this should all be one continuous plane in Youth and as we get increasingly complex with age you can often restore it simply by connecting across these disrupting planes or lines that form over time to bring it all together we also did a little bit of neatening up of the structure of the nose and a little bit of volume replacement in the lips the overall result is the that she looks fresher more youthful but also natural so where should you inject if you want to get similar results as what I've shown in this before and after the first thing is don't forget patient selection not all patients will respond the same way and often it is easier to treat people who have a good underlying structure they don't have excess volume on their face and typically it's a little bit easier in longer faces rather than rounder faces patient selection is key often clinicians and patients are disheartened by results and questions when in fact it was never possible with the modalities at hand to get the type of result they

were after so think carefully about your starting point to better assess the chance of you achieving a great result if you have a patient who's suitable which means a relatively small gou with good bone structure and areas where there's clearly room to add volume without causing a disproportionate or disharmonious result then you're on a good position to start treatment planning always starts with treating the upper or the mid-phase first you get most potential of lifting when you are above a structure so for example the cheek or the zygoma I put a lot of time and effort into sculpting above the zygoma which is the area where you are most likely to be projecting tissue away from the gel and therefore creating a support for potential lifting later on now wait a second I know many of you will be thinking filler doesn't lift filler fills this is a very common statement that many good injectors will say to their patients however it's not strictly true in fact it's very easy to prove that volume within a structure actually

creates the potential for lifting there are many examples of this throughout engineering but also even in the human body now this is a little bit left field but there is one structure in the human body that when you inject fluid into it lifts that's right I don't know if I should say it on this the Corpus kosum is a structure within the penis that when it fills with blood as it extends it lifts so this is proving that fluid can lift a structure it is the combination of both fluid and structure that is important and this is where understanding the anatomy of the face can also help our face is held onto the bone with ligaments and the ligaments run through the smass so if you have firm connective tissue attached to the bone with a ligament that is sagged down due to Lost volume and you replace that volume it is a fact looking at the geometry that this will cause a small lift this happens most around the cheek but similar principles apply when injecting around the jow and the chin to cause stretching of tissue that causes a

subtle lift it's no replacement for a facelift but it's an important result in the right cohort of patients and you can use the word lift as long as you cave at it with the fact that is relatively subtle this is not the type of lift that will replace face lifts but it's certainly impactful I think you can see this in some of the best results to illustrate the potential for lifting have a look at this simple video using a model in this model I have recreated parts of the anatomy to show you the mechanics of how this can work on the first layer there is bone and in the second layer there is smass the smass is attached to the bone with string and underneath that is the deep fat pad as you can see as it is inflated nobody argues against the idea that filler can project what this diagram will show you is that as it projects if the ligaments are facing downwards the only way they can affect the smass is for it to lift what is beneath this is the mechanics of a potential lift from dermal filler underneath the smass proven in a mathematical way so the bottom layer

here is perspex it's connected with string to the next layer of perspex and underneath that is a beach ball which will inflate with some air as I turn the pump on you will see the ball being inflated which pushes the smass away from the bone and that causes elevation of the smass as you can see it lifts up down to the ligaments which force this elev it's this combination of structure and volume that causes a lift and this is why I believe you can get a subtle lift when treating cheeks in some patients in other parts of the face you can also contribute to the sense or the actual lifting of the gou now particularly laterally when treating the posterior aspect of the jawline you're adding volume to an area where there's often relatively dramatic volume loss if you look at many older patients you will see volume lust is quite dramatic laterally and then the medial cheek stays full while the mid face shrinks so then you have a dominant midcheek fat pad which swings forward because there's nothing supporting the skin from behind and

nothing supporting it in the front so simply by adding volume both anteriorly and posteriorly as well as superiorly in the cheek you've replaced a lot of the volume that has depleted naturally with age to create a more harmonious and more structured form so where do we inject to create this enhancement the first thing is I think you get much of the best lifting from injecting deep underneath the smass and this makes sense from an anatomical perspective as you project smass away and the ligaments are holding onto it you will get subtle elevation similarly when injecting the jawline particularly the gonial angle I find it's more aesthetically pleasing to inject underneath the muscle onto the periosteum and this would in theory also have a similar effect on the lower face which is everything is slightly supported by having volume underneath as soon as you in the hypodermis it's a little bit less effective but of course the skin still has these connections to the ligaments and to the Bone so you can still get some improvement in terms of support but a lot of the Improvement that you see at this level is simply about blending in imperfections creating

more subtle planes instead of being disrupted by shifts in fat pads they are all becoming part of one structure let's consider now the Buckle area why would you inject underneath the zygoma in this tricky area this is a very common area in Slimmer people that gets thinner as you get older and then you can see an emptiness or A disruption to that connecting plane and it's quite a rewarding although it does take a larger amount of volume because there's no support underneath I think when you inject deep on bone you get better bang for your buck in many cases but as there is no bone to support filler here expect to use larger amounts one to two Mills either side is quite common the reason to do it is to get rid of that sense of gauntness or emptiness it's a big part of Youth and Beauty to have a simple lower cheek that blends harmoniously in with the upper cheek and it will also cause a decrease in those accordion lines that you get when you smile now what about the need for surgery of course non-surgical techniques cannot solve all these problems and often the patient will get a better result with surgical options our job is always to

consult our patients in a way that they have all the information available to make the best decision that they can and what we'd be looking for is a cohort of patients who want bigger results or more perfect results or longer lasting results and also have an appetite for surgery I know in my practice many patients will not even consider facial as Aesthetic Surgery and tend to think only about non-surgical options and for these patients you may try harder in cases where some of your colleagues may say they clearly need surgery for me it's always been about the patient and what they want and empowering them to make decisions within their value framework to learn more about how to create results like this using a new framework which I've developed make sure you go to the description in this video for links to my training
